org.docgene.model

Class XMLArticle

  • java.lang.Object
    • org.docgene.model.AbstractXMLParent
      • org.docgene.model.AbstractXMLParentArticle
        • org.docgene.model.XMLArticle
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    java.lang.Comparable<Article>, Article, org.docgene.model.globals.PackagedElement, org.docgene.model.globals.RootElement, org.docgene.model.globals.XMLRootElement, HTMLOutput, XMLParent, XMLParentArticle
    Direct Known Subclasses:
    ArticlesGlossary, XMLIndex

    public class XMLArticle
extends AbstractXMLParentArticle
implements java.lang.Comparable<Article>
    Represents an XML article.

    Article reference

    If no article ID is defined the article description will be used for the ID. If an article ID is defined the article can be both retrieved via its description or its ID.

    Examples

     <article id="articleID" desc="article description">
 <par>
 This is a paragraph.
 </par>
 <cat id="category" />
 </article>
    This article can be referenced by its ID or its description. These references are all valid:
    • <ref id="articleID" desc="the description" />
    • <ref id="article description" desc="the description" />
    • <ref id="article_description" desc="the description" />
     <article desc="article description">
 <par>
 This is a paragraph.
 </par>
 <cat id="category" />
 </article>
    This article can be referenced by its description only because it has no ID. These two references are valid:
    • <ref id="article description" desc="the description" />
    • <ref id="article_description" desc="the description" />

    • Constructor Detail

      • XMLArticle

        public XMLArticle(java.lang.String desc,
                  boolean keepCase)
        Constructor.
        Parameters:
        desc - the article description. The article getID() will be equal to its description
        keepCase - true if the article description should keep the title description case

      • XMLArticle

        public XMLArticle(java.lang.String id,
                  java.lang.String desc,
                  boolean keepCase)
        Constructor.
        Parameters:
        id - the article id
        desc - the article description
        keepCase - true if the article description should keep the title description case

      • XMLArticle

        public XMLArticle(java.lang.String desc,
                  boolean keepCase,
                  boolean isConcurrent)
        Constructor.
        Parameters:
        desc - the article description. The article getID() will be equal to its description
        keepCase - true if the article description should keep the title description case
        isConcurrent - true for concurrent parsing

        public XMLArticle(java.lang.String id,
                  java.lang.String desc,
                  boolean keepCase,
                  boolean isConcurrent)
        Constructor.

        desc - the article description
        keepCase - true if the article description should keep the title description case
        isConcurrent - true for a concurrent parsing

        Return the number of titles and chapters currently defined in the Article.

        this article

      • getSearchableTitles

        public java.util.Map<java.lang.String,TitledElement> getSearchableTitles()
        Return the map of Searchable titles. Note that for these titles:
        • "Notes" titles are not taken into account
        • "SeeAlso" titles are not taken into account
        • Duplicate titles are not taken into account
        Specified by:
        getSearchableTitles in interface XMLParentArticle
        Returns:
        the map of Searchable titles

      • hasSearchableTitles

        public boolean hasSearchableTitles()
        Return true if the article has searchable titles.
        Specified by:
        hasSearchableTitles in interface Article
        Returns:
        true if the article has searchable titles

        public boolean hasAnchors()
        Return true if the article has anchors.
        Specified by:
        hasAnchors in interface XMLParentArticle
        Returns:
        true if the article has anchors

      • addTitle

        public void addTitle(TitledElement title,
                     boolean allowIdenticalTitles)
        Add a title to the article.
        Parameters:
        title - the title element
        allowIdenticalTitles - true if two titles with the same title are allowed

      • addResolvedTitle

        public void addResolvedTitle(XMLTitle title,
                             boolean allowIdenticalTitles)
        Add a resolved title to the article. This title may be directly defined in the article, or it can result from another included article.
        Parameters:
        title - the title
        allowIdenticalTitles - true if two titles with the same title are allowed

      • addResolvedChapter

        public void addResolvedChapter(XMLChapter chapter,
                               boolean allowIdenticalTitles)
        Add a resolved chapter to the article. This title may be directly defined in the article, or it can result from another included article.
        Parameters:
        chapter - the chapter
        allowIdenticalTitles - true if two titles with the same title are allowed

      • addTitle

        public void addTitle(XMLTitle title,
                     boolean includeInSearch,
                     boolean allowIdenticalTitles)
        Add a resolved title to the article. This title may be directly defined in the article, or it can result from another included article.
        Parameters:
        title - the title
        includeInSearch - true if the title must be included in the Search box
        allowIdenticalTitles - true if two titles with the same title are allowed

      • addChapter

        public void addChapter(XMLChapter chapter,
                       boolean includeInSearch,
                       boolean allowIdenticalTitles)
        Add a resolved chapter to the article. This title may be directly defined in the article, or it can result from another included article.
        Parameters:
        chapter - the chapter
        includeInSearch - true if the title must be included in the Search box
        allowIdenticalTitles - true if two titles with the same title are allowed

      • hasTitles

        public boolean hasTitles()
        Return true if the article has at least one title.
        Returns:
        true if the article has at least one title

      • addAnchor

        public void addAnchor(AnchorElement anchor)
        Add an Anchor to the article.
        Parameters:
        anchor - the Anchor

        public boolean hasAnchorElement(java.lang.String id)
        Return true if the article has a titled element of a specified ID.
        Parameters:
        id - the titledelement ID
        Returns:
        true if the article has a titled element of the specified ID

        public void setTitledElementsList(java.util.List<TitledElement> titledEltsList)
        Set the ordered list of titled elements (chapters or titles).
        Parameters:
        titledEltsList - the ordered list of titled elements (chapters or titles)

      • getTitledElementsList

        public java.util.List<TitledElement> getTitledElementsList()
        Return the ordered list of titled elements (chapters or titles).
        Specified by:
        getTitledElementsList in interface XMLParentArticle
        Returns:
        the ordered list of titled elements

      • isTitlesResolved

        public boolean isTitlesResolved()
        Return true if the titles has been resolved. Titles resolution is performed when titles coming from included articles has been takne into account.
        Returns:
        true if the titles has been resolved

      • resolveTitles

        public void resolveTitles()
        Tag the titles as resolved.

      • getTitlesOrIncludes

        public java.util.List<TitleOrIncludeElement> getTitlesOrIncludes()
        Return the list of titles and XMLInclude elements.

        the list of titles and XMLInclude elements

      • hasTitledElementsList

        public boolean hasTitledElementsList()
        Return true if there is a list of titled elements (chapters or titles).
        Specified by:
        hasTitledElementsList in interface XMLParentArticle
        Returns:
        true if there is a list of titled elements (chapters or titles)

      • getAnchorElements

        public java.util.Map<java.lang.String,AnchorElement> getAnchorElements()
        Return all the anchor elements of the articles sorted by their title. It contains the xml "anchor" elements, but also the titles.
        Specified by:
        getAnchorElements in interface XMLParentArticle
        Returns:
        the anchor elements

      • getAnchors

        public java.util.Map<java.lang.String,XMLAnchor> getAnchors()
        Return all the "anchor" elements of the articles sorted by their title.
        Specified by:
        getAnchors in interface XMLParentArticle
        Returns:
        the "anchor" elements

        Parameters:
        id - the anchor ID

        the AnchorElement

        hasAnchor in interface XMLParentArticle
        Parameters:
        id - the anchor ID
        Returns:
        true if the article has an Anchor of the specified title
